config.systemPrompt:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose the smallest relevant tool set needed for the requested evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Verify only material uncertainty, and reuse evidence already collected instead of repeating equivalent investigation.
- When the available evidence is sufficient, stop investigating and provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

methodology: One read-only external-project analysis task executed through a DeepSeek LLM agent; it is operationally measured but not success-scored.
with_lynx: LLM has access only to the task-specific LYNX tool profile plus read_file.
without_lynx: LLM has access to read_file + grep only.
Fresh conversation per task x condition x run. No shared history or cache between conditions.
Paid microbenchmark protocol: exact deepseek-v4-flash, temperature 0, fixed per-round seeds, identical prompts/order/limits/runtime; no model or provider fallback.
Mode: live API calls. Suite: external-project.
Cost is estimated from token usage and configured pricing. Wall time includes API latency.
External-project budget: each condition is capped at 8 tool calls per task to prevent an unsupported workflow from consuming unbounded paid context.
ROI claims blocked when baseline invalid or sample size too small.

messages.content:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose the smallest relevant tool set needed for the requested evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Verify only material uncertainty, and reuse evidence already collected instead of repeating equivalent investigation.
- When the available evidence is sufficient, stop investigating and provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

I'm doing a lynx-fastapi cleanup sprint. Find symbols (functions, classes) in the codebase that appear to have zero callers and zero usages — these are candidates for removal. List at least 5 concrete examples with their file paths and names. Do NOT invent symbols. Only report symbols you have verified are defined in the codebase, and stop once 5 well-supported candidates are established. Respond with JSON: {"unused_symbols":[{"name":"...","file":"...","kind":"function|class"}], "evidence":["..."], "summary":"..."}.

evidence: find_dead_code confirmed zero incoming CALLS, USAGE, READS, and TESTS edges for all five symbols across the entire graph
get_code_snippet verified each symbol is actually defined in the source (not a stale index entry)
All five are non-exported (is_exported=false), meaning they are not part of the public API surface
build_middleware_stack (fastapi/applications.py:1020-1068) is a 49-line override of Starlette's middleware stack builder — it calls some functions but nothing calls it
setup (fastapi/applications.py:1105-1158) registers OpenAPI, Swagger UI, and ReDoc routes internally but has no external callers
get_websocket_app (fastapi/routing.py:748-782) builds a WebSocket ASGI app from a Dependant but is never referenced anywhere
websocket_session (fastapi/routing.py:157-178) wraps a WebSocket coroutine into an ASGI app but has zero callers
make_not_authenticated_error (fastapi/security/oauth2.py:401-421) creates a 401 HTTPException with Bearer challenge but is never called, even within the OAuth2 class itself
result.summary: Found 5 well-supported dead-code candidates in the FastAPI codebase. These are all non-exported functions/methods with zero incoming graph references (no callers, no usages, no reads, no tests). The most impactful removal candidate by line count is setup (54 lines) and build_middleware_stack (49 lines) in applications.py, while get_websocket_app (35 lines), websocket_session (22 lines), and make_not_authenticated_error (21 lines) provide smaller wins but equal confidence. All are safe for a cleanup sprint since they are not part of the public API.

You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.

- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.

I'm doing a lynx-fastapi cleanup sprint. Find symbols (functions, classes) in the codebase that appear to have zero callers and zero usages — these are candidates for removal. List at least 5 concrete examples with their file paths and names. Do NOT invent symbols. Only report symbols you have verified are defined in the codebase, and stop once 5 well-supported candidates are established. Respond with JSON: {"unused_symbols":[{"name":"...","file":"...","kind":"function|class"}], "evidence":["..."], "summary":"..."}.

evidence: is_body_allowed_for_status_code is defined at fastapi/utils.py line 26 but is not imported or called in fastapi/routing.py, fastapi/applications.py, fastapi/openapi/utils.py, or fastapi/dependencies/utils.py (the main utility consumers).

generate_encoders_by_class_tuples is defined at fastapi/encoders.py line 115 and called only once at line 126 of the same file. It is not re-exported from fastapi/__init__.py and no other module imports it.
encoders_by_class_tuples is a module-level variable at fastapi/encoders.py line 126 and is only referenced inside jsonable_encoder (line 336) in the same file. It is never imported externally.
_UjsonModule protocol class at fastapi/responses.py line 16 is only used for a local type cast on the optional ujson import (line 49). No external references exist.
_OrjsonModule protocol class at fastapi/responses.py line 20 is only used for a local type cast on the optional orjson import (line 57). No external references exist.
result.summary: Found 5 symbols (3 functions, 2 classes) in the fastapi/ source directory that are defined but have zero callers or usages outside their own defining module. These are safe candidates for removal in a cleanup sprint.
